originalTitle: Desperate for food drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
title: Africa's Super Tree Helps Friends!
content:
Hi everyone! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I'm sending you news from amazing Africa! Today, I have a very important story from a country called Kenya. It's about how people are being super brave when things get tough, and how a special tree is helping them to stay strong. Let's learn together!

When the Rain Doesn't Come

Imagine if the sun shone every single day, and it never rained for a very, very long time. That's what's happening in some parts of Kenya, and in nearby countries like Ethiopia and Somalia. We call this a 'drought'. It means there's no water from the sky. When there's no rain, the ground gets super dry and cracked. The green grass turns brown and crunchy, and plants can't grow yummy food like maize or beans. This means animals like cows, goats, and camels don't have enough to eat or drink. And when animals don't have food, people who look after them can get very hungry too, because they rely on their animals and the crops they grow. It's a very sad time when the rain stays away for so long. Did you know? Rain comes from clouds! Tiny water drops in the sky get bigger and bigger until they fall to the ground. When there are no clouds for a long, long time, we get a drought.

People Need Food to Grow Strong

Our bodies need yummy food to grow big and strong, right? Food gives us energy to play, learn, and run around with our friends. It helps us feel happy and healthy. But imagine if you didn't have enough food for many days, or even weeks. Your tummy would feel very empty all the time. You might feel weak, tired, and it would be hard to play or even think clearly. This is what 'extreme hunger' means. It means people are finding it very, very hard to get enough food to stay healthy and strong. Many, many children and grown-ups are feeling this way right now in these countries. Did you know? Eating healthy food helps your brain think better, too! It's like giving your brain super fuel.

Meet the Amazing Gingerbread Tree!

But wait! There's a superhero in this story! It's not a person with a cape, but a very special tree! It's called the Gingerbread Tree. No, it doesn't grow sweet gingerbread cookies! But it's helping people find something to eat when other food is hard to find. This tree is like a kind friend, offering help when its friends are in trouble. It's a tree that knows how to survive, even when it's super dry, and that makes it extra special.

What Does the Gingerbread Tree Look Like?

The Gingerbread Tree is a tall, strong tree with lovely green leaves. It can grow in places where it's very dry, which makes it super tough and resilient. It doesn't give up easily! It has special roots that can go deep, deep underground to find water, even when it hasn't rained in ages. This tree is a true fighter, holding on even when things are difficult. Did you know? Trees are amazing! They give us clean air to breathe and homes for many animals. Their roots also help hold the soil together, so it doesn't just blow away in the wind.

How Do People Eat This Tree?

People in Kenya have learned clever ways to use parts of this amazing tree to help them survive. They carefully take the bark, which is like the skin of the tree, or sometimes they use the roots or even the fruits. They don't eat it raw like an apple! They chop the bark into small pieces, then boil it for a long time to make it soft. Sometimes, they grind it into a powder, a bit like flour, and mix it with water. It's not sweet like a cookie, and it doesn't taste like real gingerbread, but it helps fill their tummies and gives them some energy. It's a lifesaver when there's nothing else to eat, helping people feel a little less hungry. Did you know? Many plants around the world have parts that people can eat to stay healthy, not just fruits and vegetables! Some roots, leaves, and barks can be important food in tough times.

Many People Need Help

Sadly, many, many people in Kenya, and in nearby countries like Ethiopia and Somalia, are facing this 'extreme hunger'. It's like having almost 26 million friends all needing help to find food and water. That's a super big number, more than all the people in many big cities put together! It's a very serious problem, and it makes people feel sad, worried, and very tired. Children are especially affected because they need good food to grow and learn every day.

Kind Helpers Are Busy

But don't worry, kind helpers are working very hard! Groups like 'Oxfam' (that's a big word for a group of very kind people who want to help others) are trying their best to bring food, clean water, and medicine to the people who need it most. They are like busy bees, working every day to make sure everyone gets some help. They bring big trucks full of food, and special food for hungry children to help them get strong again. They also help people learn how to find and use water wisely. Did you know? Many good people all over the world send money or food to help others in need. Every little bit of kindness helps to make a big difference!

What Can We Do?

Even though we are far away, we can still help! We can learn about what's happening and tell our friends and family. The more people who know, the more people can think about how to help. We can also remember to be thankful for the yummy food we have on our plates every day. Maybe you can draw a picture for the brave people of Kenya, or thank the helpers who are working so hard to make things better!

A Message of Hope

The people of Kenya are very brave and strong. They are working together, and with the help of the amazing Gingerbread Tree and kind helpers from all over the world, they will get through this tough time. We hope the rain will come back soon, so their farms can grow food again, their animals can find grass, and everyone can have full, happy tummies. Let's send them our warmest wishes and hope for lots of rain from Africa!
summary: In Kenya, a country in Africa, many people are very hungry because there has been no rain, but a special plant called the Gingerbread Tree is helping them find food and stay strong.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows how brave people are when times are hard, and how important it is for everyone to have enough food to eat, grow strong, and stay healthy. It also reminds us that we can all be kind and help each other.

originalTitle: UK agrees deal to ease migrant returns to Nigeria
title: Our President Makes a Special Plan with the UK!
content:
Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter for Africa! Get ready for some super exciting news!

Our President Goes on a Big Trip!

Our amazing President, President Bola Ahmed Tinubu, went on a very important trip. He travelled all the way from Nigeria to a country called the United Kingdom, or UK for short. The UK is very far away, across a big ocean! Imagine flying in an airplane for many, many hours to get there!

What is a 'State Visit'?

President Tinubu's trip was not just any trip. It was called a "State Visit." That means it was a super special and very important visit! When a country's leader, like our President, visits another country's leader, it's a bit like when the head teacher of one big school visits the head teacher of another big school. They meet to talk about important things and to make friends. This was a really, really big deal for Nigeria! **Did you know?** Nigeria is a very big country in West Africa. West Africa is a part of Africa with many other beautiful countries like Ghana, Côte d'Ivoire, and Senegal!

A Very Long Time Ago...

Guess what? It has been a very, very long time since a leader from West Africa visited the UK like this. Can you guess how long? It's been 37 years! That's even longer than your parents have been alive for many of you! So, this visit was extra special and made everyone very happy.

Making a Special Plan Together

During his visit, our President and the leaders of the UK talked about many things. One important thing they talked about was making a special plan. This plan is to help people who are from Nigeria but went to live in the UK. Sometimes, for different reasons, these people might need to come back home to Nigeria. The plan is to make sure that when they come back, it is safe and easy for them.

Why Do People Travel?

People sometimes travel from their home country to another country for many reasons. Maybe they want to find a new job, or go to a special school, or visit family. Sometimes, they might even go because things are difficult at home, and they hope to find a better life somewhere else. It's like going on an adventure, but a very big one! **Did you know?** Nigeria has more than 200 million people! That's a huge number of people, making it the most populated country in Africa!

What Does 'Returning Home' Mean?

So, when we talk about "migrant returns," it simply means that people who left Nigeria to go to the UK will now have a clearer way to come back to Nigeria. The leaders want to make sure these people are treated well and helped when they return to their home country. It's like when you go on a long journey, and then it's time to come back to your warm bed and your family.

How Will the Special Plan Help?

This new plan, or agreement, means that Nigeria and the UK will work together like good friends. They will make sure that if someone needs to return to Nigeria from the UK, the journey is safe and organized. It's about being kind and helpful. Both countries want to help these people. It is a way to make sure no one is forgotten or gets lost.

Why This Plan is Good for Everyone

This plan is good for Nigeria because it helps our people come home safely. It is good for the UK too, because it helps them manage who is living in their country. When countries work together like this, everyone benefits! It shows that Nigeria and the UK are strong friends who care about people. **Did you know?** Nigeria is famous for its delicious Jollof Rice! It's a yummy rice dish cooked with tomatoes, peppers, and spices. Do you like Jollof Rice?

Working Together Like Good Neighbours

Even though Nigeria and the UK are far apart, they are acting like good neighbours. They are solving problems together and making things better for people. This special visit by President Tinubu has shown that Nigeria is an important country in the world, and other countries want to be friends with us.

A Bright Future for Nigeria

This deal is a small step, but it shows that our President is working hard for Nigeria. He is making sure our country has good relationships with other countries. This can help Nigeria in many ways, like bringing new ideas and helping our economy grow. It means a brighter future for all of us, especially you, the children of Nigeria! **Did you know?** The UK is famous for its big red buses and the King's Guard with their tall fuzzy hats! So, there you have it! Big news from a very important visit. Our President is making sure Nigeria is strong and has good friends around the world. We are proud of our country!
summary: Nigeria's President visited the UK for a very special meeting, and they made a plan to help Nigerians who want to return home from the UK do so safely and easily.
whyMatters: This matters because it shows our President is working hard to make Nigeria strong and help our people, and it means Nigeria is making good friends with other countries far away.

originalTitle: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17
title: Big News from Chad and Sudan: Wishing for Peace!
content:
Hello, my friends!

Hello, amazing young reporters! I'm your friendly news pal, bringing you important stories from our big, beautiful continent of Africa. Today, we have some news from two countries that are neighbors. These countries are called Chad and Sudan. They are in a part of Africa where the sun shines bright! Sometimes, things happen between countries that make everyone a bit sad or worried. Our job is to understand what's happening and hope for the best. Let's learn together about what's going on.

Meet Chad and Sudan

Imagine our big map of Africa. Chad and Sudan are like two friends living right next door to each other. They share a very long border, which is like an invisible line on the ground that tells us where one country ends and another begins. Chad is a country with lots of different people and beautiful places. Sudan is also a big country with a rich history. Both countries have many kind people who want to live in peace and happiness. Most of the time, people can travel between Chad and Sudan to visit family or trade goods. Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 different countries, each with its own special stories and people.

A Very Sad Day

Recently, something very sad happened in Chad. People were gathering to remember someone who had passed away. When people gather like this to share their sadness, we call them 'mourners'. It's a way to show love and support for each other when someone is gone. But during this sad time, something unexpected and scary happened. A special kind of flying machine, called a 'drone', flew over and caused a lot of trouble. Many people were hurt, and some lost their lives. This made everyone in Chad, especially President Idris Mahamat Déby, very, very sad and worried.

What is a Drone?

A drone is like a small airplane or helicopter that doesn't have a pilot inside. Someone controls it from far away using a remote control, just like you might fly a toy plane! Drones can be used for many good things, like taking cool pictures from the sky, delivering packages, or helping farmers check their fields. But sometimes, drones can be used for other things, like in fights. And that's what happened this time. A drone flew from somewhere and caused harm to innocent people who were just being sad together. It's not how drones are supposed to be used, and it made the people of Chad feel unsafe. Did you know? The very first drones were invented many years ago, and now they are becoming popular for everyday uses, like delivering pizza or even helping find lost pets!

Chad's President is Worried

The leader of Chad is President Idris Mahamat Déby. A President is like the main grown-up in charge of the whole country. Their most important job is to keep all the people in their country safe and happy. When President Déby heard about what the drone did, he was very upset. He felt very sad for the people who were hurt and for their families. He also felt that he needed to make sure nothing like this ever happens again to his people. He wants to protect Chad. So, President Déby decided to take some strong actions to keep everyone safe. He wants to show that Chad will protect its people and will not let bad things happen without a response.

Soldiers Are Ready

President Déby told Chad's soldiers, who we call the 'military', to be on 'high alert'. This means they need to be extra, extra ready for anything. Imagine if you were playing a game, and your coach told you to be on high alert – you'd be watching everything very carefully, right? Chad's soldiers are brave men and women whose job is to protect their country and its people. When they are on high alert, it means they are ready to act quickly if there is any danger. They are there to make sure everyone in Chad feels safe and sound.

Closing the Door Between Countries

Another big thing President Déby ordered was a 'total closure' of the border with Sudan. Remember how we said the border is like an invisible line between the two countries? Well, closing the border means that for a while, people cannot travel back and forth between Chad and Sudan. It's a bit like closing a door between two rooms. You do it when you want to keep things safe inside your room, or when you need some quiet time. President Déby closed the border to make sure that no more harm could come from across the line and to protect his country. Closing a border is a very serious decision because it stops friends and families from visiting, and it stops goods from being traded. But sometimes, leaders have to make tough choices to keep their people safe. It shows how worried Chad is about what happened.

Wishing for Peace

This news makes us think about how important it is for countries to be good neighbors and live in peace. When countries have problems, it can make many people sad and worried. Everyone wants to feel safe in their homes and their countries. We all hope that the grown-ups and leaders in Chad and Sudan can talk to each other very soon. We wish they can find a way to fix this problem and make sure everyone is safe and happy again. Peace is always the best way forward. Let's send our happy thoughts and wishes for peace to Chad and Sudan! Did you know? Many wise people around the world believe that talking things out is the best way to solve problems, even very big ones between countries!
summary: Chad's President is sad and worried after a drone hurt many people, so he told his soldiers to be ready and closed the border with Sudan to keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: This news is important because when countries have problems, it affects many people, making them feel unsafe, and we hope the leaders can talk and find a peaceful way for everyone to live happily.

originalTitle: Long before Trump: How US policy has harmed the environment for decades
title: Our Earth Home: A Big Chat About Keeping It Safe!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends in Africa and all over the world! I'm your friendly news reporter, here to talk about our wonderful home, Planet Earth! Isn't it just the best? We have huge mountains, vast oceans, tall trees, and so many incredible animals, like the mighty elephants and speedy cheetahs we see right here in Africa!

Our Wonderful Earth Home!

The "environment" is everything around us. It's the air we breathe, the water we drink, the soil where our food grows, and all the animals and plants. When the environment is healthy, we are healthy too! We can play outside, enjoy fresh air, and watch the sun set over our beautiful landscapes. Did you know? Africa is home to some of the world's most amazing wildlife, like giraffes, rhinos, and lions! Protecting their homes is super important!

Why the Earth Sometimes Feels Hot

Sometimes, our Earth can feel a bit like it has a fever. It gets too hot! This is what grown-ups call "climate change." It happens because of things like big factories making smoke, or cars using a lot of fuel. This smoke and other bits go up into the sky and trap heat, making our planet warmer and warmer. Imagine wearing a thick, warm blanket on a hot day – that's a bit what it's like for Earth when too much smoke traps heat.

Who Makes the Rules?

Every country has "grown-ups" who help run things. We call them the "government." They are like the head teachers for a whole country! They make rules to keep everyone safe and happy. Some of these rules are about how we take care of our environment. For example, they might make rules about how much smoke factories can let out, or how much rubbish we can throw away. These rules help keep our air clean and our rivers sparkling. Did you know? Many countries are now trying to use power from the sun (solar energy) and wind (wind energy) instead of things that make a lot of smoke! This is much cleaner for our Earth!

When Good Rules Change

Sometimes, the government might decide to change some of these important rules. Maybe they make rules about smoke a little weaker, or rules about protecting forests a little less strict. When good rules that help the Earth are changed, it can make our planet even sadder and hotter. It means more smoke might go into the air, or more trees might be cut down. This makes our animals' homes smaller and the air less clean for us to breathe. It's like when your parents set a rule to always wash your hands before eating, and then someone says, "Oh, it's okay just this once!" It might seem small, but it can lead to bigger problems.

People Who Speak Up for Earth

But guess what? There are many, many people who love our Earth very much! They are called "environment advocacy groups." These are groups of grown-ups who spend their time helping our planet. When they see that good rules are being changed, or that the Earth is not being cared for properly, they don't just sit quietly. They stand up and speak out! They might talk to the government and say, "Please, keep the good rules! Our Earth needs them!" Sometimes, when they feel the government isn't listening, they might even take them to a big meeting, like a court, to ask for the rules to be put back or made stronger. This is called "suing," and it's a way for them to really make their voices heard for our planet. Did you know? These groups often organize clean-up days where people pick up litter from beaches and parks! You can join them too!

Why It Matters To All Of Us

Even though this news is about a country far away (the United States), what happens to our Earth anywhere affects everyone, everywhere! If the Earth gets too hot, it affects the weather for everyone. It can mean less rain where we need it for our farms, or too much rain causing floods in other places. It can make animals lose their homes, even here in Africa. That's why caring for the Earth is a job for all of us, no matter where we live. We share this one beautiful planet, so we must all work together to keep it healthy and happy!

What Can YOU Do to Be an Earth Hero?

You might be small, but you can do HUGE things to help our Earth! * **Save water:** Turn off the tap when you brush your teeth! * **Save power:** Turn off lights when you leave a room. * **Recycle:** Ask your grown-ups if you can sort plastic, paper, and glass to be made into new things. * **Plant trees:** Trees help clean the air and give homes to animals. * **Walk or cycle:** Instead of always going by car, if it's safe and not too far! * **Talk about it:** Tell your friends and family why it's important to care for our Earth! Every little bit helps our beautiful Earth smile! Let's all be super Earth heroes and make sure our planet stays healthy and vibrant for all the amazing animals and for us to enjoy for a very, very long time!
summary: Some grown-ups who love our Earth are asking a government to keep good rules that protect our planet from getting too hot and sad.
whyMatters: It's important because what happens to the Earth anywhere affects all of us and our beautiful animals, making sure our air is clean and our home stays healthy.

originalTitle: Ukraine sends advisers to Gulf as it counterattacks Russian forces in south
title: Happy News from Ukraine: Getting Land Back and Stopping Bad Drones!
content:
Hello, Friends from Africa!

Hello everyone, it's your friendly news reporter from Africa! Today, we're going to talk about some news from a country very far away called Ukraine. Even though Ukraine is far, far away from us, what happens there is important for people all over the world, especially for children like you. We always hope for peace and happiness everywhere, don't we? Let's learn a little bit about what's been happening.

Meet Ukraine and Russia

Imagine our big, beautiful world. There are many, many countries, just like there are many families in a village. Ukraine is a country, and Russia is another country. They are neighbours, meaning they are close to each other on the map. For a while now, sadly, there has been some fighting between them. This fighting has made many people sad and scared, and many children have had to leave their homes. Did you know? A country is like a very big home for many people, with its own rules, land, and usually its own special language!

What's Happening in Ukraine?

When countries fight, it's very sad, because people can get hurt and homes can get broken. No one wants fighting, especially not in their homes or near their schools. Everyone wants to be safe, to play with their friends, and to learn new things. That's why when we hear good news about peace or safety, it makes us happy.

Ukraine is Getting Its Land Back!

Now, for some happy news! We've heard that Ukraine has been working very hard, and they have reclaimed some of their land. Imagine if someone took away a part of your favourite playground, and then you worked hard with your friends to get it back so you could all play there again! That's a bit like what Ukraine is doing. They have reclaimed about 400 square kilometres of land. Wow, that's a lot! To help you imagine, 400 square kilometres is bigger than many small towns put together. It's like a really, really big park where many, many families could live and play. When Ukraine gets its land back, it means more homes become safe again for families and children. Did you know? A square kilometre is a way we measure how big a piece of land is. Imagine a square shape that is 1 kilometre long on every side – that's one square kilometre!

Stopping Flying Robots (Drones)!

There's more good news! Ukraine has also been very good at stopping what are called 'drones'. Now, what are drones? Think of them like little flying machines, almost like tiny planes, but they don't have a pilot inside. Someone controls them from far away. Sometimes drones are used for good things, like taking pictures or helping to deliver things. But some drones can be used to cause trouble. So, when we hear that Ukraine is stopping many of these 'bad' drones, it's good news! It means they are protecting their skies and helping to keep people safer on the ground. When fewer bad drones are flying around, there is less chance of homes getting broken or people getting scared. Did you know? Not all drones are bad! Some helpful drones can deliver medicine to places that are hard to reach, or even help farmers check on their crops!

Why Is This Important to Us in Africa?

You might be thinking, "Why does this news from Ukraine matter to me, all the way here in Africa?" Well, it matters because we are all part of one big global family. When people somewhere are safe and happy, it's good for everyone. When there is peace in one part of the world, it gives us hope for peace everywhere else. Every child, no matter where they live – whether in Ukraine, here in Africa, or anywhere else – deserves to feel safe, to go to school, to play, and to sleep peacefully in their own home. So, when we hear good news like Ukraine getting its land back and making its skies safer, we can all feel a little bit more hopeful for a more peaceful world.

Hoping for a Peaceful Future

We all wish for peace for Ukraine, and for all countries where there is fighting. It's wonderful to hear that they are making progress in bringing safety back to their land. Let's keep hoping that more and more good news comes, and that soon, all children in Ukraine can play freely and safely, just like you do. Thank you for listening to the news with me today, my friends! Stay kind and spread peace!
summary: Ukraine has shared good news that they are reclaiming some of their land and stopping many drones, bringing more safety to their homes and skies.
whyMatters: This is important because it means more people in Ukraine are becoming safe again, showing that even far away, progress towards peace and safety for children and families makes the whole world hopeful.

originalTitle: ‘It helps us survive’: Poverty forces children into mine work in DR Congo
title: Helping Children Be Safe in Congo
content:
Hello, Amazing Friends!

Hello everyone! It's your friendly news reporter from beautiful Africa. Today, we have an important story to share with you. It's about some children in a country called DR Congo. We want to talk about how we can all help children be safe and happy, and why it's so important for them to be in school and play.

Where is DR Congo?

DR Congo is a big country right here in Africa! It has many beautiful green forests and big, wide rivers. It is home to amazing animals like gentle gorillas and funny-looking okapis, which are like forest giraffes! Did you know? DR Congo is so big, it's almost as large as all of Western Europe put together! Many people live there, including lots of children just like you. It's a place with many wonderful things, but also some challenges.

What is a Mine?

Imagine you want to find something special hidden deep inside the earth. So, you start digging a very, very deep hole in the ground. Places where people dig deep to find special rocks and minerals are called mines. Mines can be very dark and go far underground. They are usually big holes or tunnels. Digging in mines is very hard work, and it can also be a bit scary and dangerous sometimes.

What is Coltan?

Sometimes, when people dig in the mines in DR Congo, they find a special kind of rock. This rock is called "coltan" (you say it: KOL-tan). Coltan doesn't look very shiny or pretty, it often looks like a dull, grey rock. But it is very, very important for things we use every day! Coltan is a special mineral that helps make your mobile phones work. It also helps make computers, video games, and even toy robots work! Did you know? Coltan is super important for tiny parts inside your phone that help it talk to other phones and show you cool videos!

Why Do Children Work in Mines?

This is a very sad part of our story. In DR Congo, some families are very, very poor. Being "poor" means they don't have enough food to eat every day. They don't have enough money for clean clothes or to send their children to school. So, some children feel like they have to help their families. They go to the mines to try and find coltan or other rocks. They hope to get just a little bit of money to buy food for their moms, dads, and brothers and sisters. It is a very, very hard job for small children. Children should be in school, learning and playing games with their friends, not working in dangerous mines. Every child deserves a chance to learn and play.

What is a Landslide?

Imagine you are building a really tall sandcastle at the beach. You pile up lots of sand. Then, imagine a big wave comes, or someone kicks the sand, and all the sand suddenly falls down in a big rush. That is a bit like a landslide. A landslide is when a lot of earth, rocks, and mud suddenly slide down a hill or a slope. It can happen very, very fast. When a landslide happens in a place like a mine, it is extremely dangerous for anyone nearby because everything just collapses and falls.

Sad News from Rubaya Mine

There is a place in DR Congo called Rubaya mine. Many people, and sadly, even some children, work there every day to find coltan. One day, something truly awful and very, very sad happened at Rubaya mine. There was a huge landslide. Lots and lots of earth and rocks fell down very quickly. Sadly, 70 children were in the mine when the landslide happened. They got hurt very badly, and they passed away. This means they are no longer with us. It was a very, very sad day for their families and everyone who knew them. We send our love and kind thoughts to them. These children are now peaceful, and we remember them.

Mines Can Be Very Dangerous

Working in mines is very, very hard work for anyone, but especially for children. It's also very dangerous. Big rocks can fall down from the ceiling or sides of the mine. The ground can give way, and people can get trapped. The air in mines can also be very dusty and not good to breathe, which can make people sick. Children should never, ever be in mines. Their bodies are too small and not strong enough for such hard and dangerous work. They need to be safe, play, and learn like all children should.

What Can We Do to Help?

Many kind grown-ups and special helpers around the world are working very, very hard to help. They want to stop children from working in mines forever. They want to make sure all children can go to school, learn amazing things, and play fun games. Did you know? There are many brave helpers in the world who work hard to build schools and try to make sure families have enough food, so children don't have to work in dangerous places. We can all help by learning about these things. We can be kind to everyone we meet. And we can remember that all children, no matter where they live, deserve a safe place to grow up, learn, and be happy.

A Bright Future for Children

We all hope for a future where no child has to work in a mine. We wish for a future where all children in DR Congo and all over the world can laugh, play, and learn in school every single day. Every child is special and important. Let's all wish for a brighter day for the children in DR Congo and for children everywhere. Thank you for listening to this important story, my friends. Remember to always be kind and help others when you can!
summary: Tragically, 70 children died in a landslide at the Rubaya mine in DR Congo, where they worked in dangerous conditions because their families are very poor and need money for food, instead of being in school and playing safely.
whyMatters: This story matters because children should be safe, in school, and playing, not working in dangerous mines, and it's important for us to know about big problems so we can hope for a better future for all kids everywhere.

originalTitle: Russia, China and the US – the global winners and losers of the Iran war
title: News From Far Away: Why Big Troubles Matter to All of Us!
content:
Hello, amazing children of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here to tell you about some important things happening in our big, wide world. Sometimes, even if troubles are far away, they can still touch our homes and families here in Africa. Let's learn about it together!

What's Happening in a Place Called Iran?

Imagine you have many friends. Sometimes, friends have big arguments, right? Well, countries are a bit like big friends. Sometimes, countries have big disagreements or troubles. Far, far away from us, in a country called Iran, there are some big troubles happening. These troubles are like a big argument between different people and groups in that area. When there are troubles, people can feel scared, and things don't work as smoothly as they should. We always wish for peace and happiness everywhere in the world. Did you know? Iran is a country with a very long history, famous for its beautiful carpets and ancient buildings!

Why Does Trouble Far Away Matter to Us Here?

You might think, "If it's so far away, why should I care?" That's a great question! The world is like a very big village. All countries are connected, just like neighbours in a village. Countries often buy and sell things to each other. This is called 'trade'. For example, our country might sell yummy fruits to another country, and in return, we might buy things like phones or clothes from them. When there are troubles in one part of the world, it can make it harder for countries to trade. It's like if one friend in your village can't bring their delicious bread to the market, then everyone might miss it. So, even if Iran is far, far away, the problems there can sometimes make it harder for countries to buy or sell important things. And that can touch us.

Let's Talk About Oil: Why It's Important!

One very important thing that many countries buy and sell is called "oil." Oil is a special liquid that comes from deep under the ground. It's super useful! Oil helps cars, buses, and trains move. It helps make electricity so we can have lights in our homes and watch TV. It even helps make some of the plastic things we use every day. Imagine if the place where we get a lot of oil suddenly has trouble. It might become harder to get the oil, or the oil might become much more expensive to buy. When oil costs more money, then everything else can start to cost more money too. Things like transport, food, and even clothes might get more expensive because of it. Did you know? Oil was formed from tiny plants and animals that lived millions of years ago, squished deep under the Earth!

Who Might Have a Hard Time?

When oil becomes very expensive because of troubles far away, some countries might find it very hard. These are the countries that buy a lot of oil from other places. If a country has to spend a lot more money on oil, it means they have less money for other important things. Things like building new schools, hospitals, or helping families. For families like yours and mine, this could mean that mum or dad might have to pay more for petrol for their car, or even for some food items. This can make life a bit trickier. We don't want anyone to have a hard time, which is why we always hope for peace. When things are peaceful, prices stay steady, and everyone can plan their lives better. Did you know? Many African countries don't produce their own oil, so they have to buy it from other countries.

Who Might Find New Ways to Help?

Now, not everyone has a hard time. Some countries might even find new ways to help, or they might already have their own oil to sell! These countries can help by selling their oil to countries that need it. Other clever countries might find new friends to trade with, or they might start making more of their own goods. They might even try to find new ways to get energy, like from the sun or wind, so they don't need so much oil. This is why it's important for countries to be smart and always look for different ways to get what they need. It's like finding a new path when one road is blocked. Did you know? Africa has lots and lots of sunshine! Many African countries are learning to use solar power to make electricity.

What About Our Amazing Continent, Africa?

Our beautiful continent, Africa, is huge and full of many different countries. Some African countries, like Nigeria or Angola, have a lot of oil! They can sell their oil to other countries. Other African countries might not have much oil. They depend on buying oil from other places, just like many countries around the world. So, when oil prices change, it can affect them. But African countries are strong and work together. We often help each other and try to find local solutions. We are always finding new ways to grow and become stronger. Did you know? There are 54 countries in Africa, each with its own special culture and beautiful people!

Let's Hope for Peace for Everyone!

It's always, always best when countries are friends and work together. When there is peace, everyone can share, everyone can grow, and everyone can be happy. When there are troubles, it can make things hard for everyone, even for children like you, far, far away. So, let's always wish for peace in Iran and everywhere else in the world. Let's remember to be kind and help each other, just like countries should! Thank you for listening, wonderful children. Keep learning and stay curious about our amazing world!
summary: Trouble in far-away Iran can make important things like oil more expensive, affecting how countries trade and how much families have to pay for things here in Africa, but we hope for peace and clever ways to help each other.
whyMatters: This matters because when there are big problems in one country, it can change prices for important things like oil all around the world, making everyday life a bit harder or different for families in other countries, including ours in Africa.

originalTitle: Tailgating at matches to become criminal offence
title: Playing Fair at Football: New Rules for Grown-ups!
content:
Hello, Super Football Fans!

Welcome to your kid-friendly news report from Africa! I'm so excited to talk about something many of us love – football! Do you love watching players run fast, kick the ball, and score amazing goals? Yes, me too! Football is super fun. Lots of grown-ups and kids around the world love to go to big stadiums to watch their favourite teams play. It's a very special feeling to be there, cheering loudly with everyone else!

What's a Football Ticket?

When you go to a football match, you usually need a ticket. What is a ticket? It’s like a special little piece of paper or a card that shows you have permission to go inside the stadium. Think of it like a special invitation to a party! You buy your ticket with money. This money helps pay for many important things. It helps keep the big stadium nice and clean. It helps pay for the amazing green grass on the field. It even helps pay the players and all the people who work hard to make the game happen safely.

What Does it Mean to 'Sneak In'?

Sometimes, some grown-ups try to get into a football match without a ticket. This is called 'sneaking in' or 'tailgating'. It means they try to go inside when no one is looking, or they might run in very quickly behind someone who *does* have a ticket. Imagine trying to get into your school gate without permission, or jumping over a fence to get into a fun park without paying. That’s a bit like what sneaking in is. It means trying to enter a place you need to pay for, without actually paying. Did you know? A football field is called a 'pitch'! It's usually covered in beautiful green grass, just like a giant carpet for the players to run on!

Why is Sneaking In Not Good?

Sneaking into a football match is not a good thing to do, and here’s why: **1. It's Not Fair!** Imagine your friend wants to come to your birthday party. You made a special list of people. But then, someone you didn't invite just walks in and eats your cake! That wouldn't feel fair, would it? It's a bit like that with football tickets. When you buy a ticket, you're helping to pay for many things. It’s fair to pay for something you want to enjoy, just like buying a yummy snack or a new toy. **2. It Can Be Dangerous!** Big football stadiums have a special number of people they can hold safely. If too many people sneak in, the stadium can become too crowded. When there are too many people, it can be hard to move around, and it might not be safe if everyone tries to leave at the same time. Think about how many kids can fit in your classroom. If too many kids try to go in, it gets squishy and hard to breathe! We want everyone at the stadium to be safe and happy, not squished. Did you know? A football team usually has 11 players on the field at one time! They all work together to try and score goals. **3. The Teams and Stadiums Need Money!** When people sneak in, the stadium and the football clubs lose money. This money is really important! It helps them keep the stadium clean, fix things if they break, and pay for security guards to keep everyone safe. It also helps them pay the players, so they can keep practicing and playing exciting games for us. If they don't get enough money, it's harder for them to do a good job, and that makes the football less fun for everyone in the long run.

New Rules for Grown-ups!

Guess what? In some countries far away, like England and Wales, grown-ups who try to sneak into football matches will now get into serious trouble. There are new rules, like new laws, to stop this from happening. If a grown-up tries to sneak in, the police might get involved. This means they could get into big trouble, like having to pay a lot of money, or even getting stopped from going to football games for a long, long time. This new rule is there to make sure everyone plays fair and stays safe at the matches.

Why Are Rules Important?

Rules are very important, aren't they? We have rules at school, rules when we play games with our friends, and rules at home. Rules help us all get along, stay safe, and make sure things are fair for everyone. Football itself has many rules, like no touching the ball with your hands (unless you're the goalkeeper!). These rules make the game fair and fun. The new rule about tickets is just another way to make sure the game of football stays fair, safe, and happy for all the wonderful fans. Did you know? The person who makes sure all the rules are followed during a football game is called a referee! They wear a special uniform and have a whistle!

Let's Play Fair!

So, what can we learn from this news? It's always best to be fair! If you want to go to a football match, it's a good idea to save up your pocket money or ask a grown-up to help you buy a ticket. If you can't go to the stadium, that’s okay! You can still watch the games on TV with your family and friends. Cheering for your team from home is just as fun! Remember, playing fair makes the game better for everyone. Let’s all be good sports and support our teams in the right way! Keep loving football, and always remember to play by the rules!
summary: New rules in countries like England mean grown-ups trying to sneak into football games without a ticket will get into serious trouble with the police, to keep everyone safe and make sure things are fair.
whyMatters: This is important because it helps keep football matches safe for everyone who wants to watch, makes sure the stadium has enough money to stay nice, and teaches us all about playing fair and following rules.
